properties.remark: REPORTED A FLIPPED CENTER PIVOT UNIT 3W OF THE 83/92 JUNCTION. TORNADO REPORT WAS ALSO IN THE VICINITY.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 21 MILES NORTHEAST OF NORTH PLATTE IN RURAL LINCOLN COUNTY. THE TORNADO TRAVELED NORTHEAST, LIFTED BRIEFLY THEN CONTINUED NORTHEAST FOR SIX MINUTES BEFORE DISSIPATING 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF STAPLETON IN SOUTHWEST LOGAN COUNTY. THE TORNADO PATH WAS 3 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 20 YARDS AND WAS RATED AN EF0.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES SOUTH SOUTHEAST OF RINGGOLD AND TRAVELED INTERMITTENTLY ON THE GROUND FOR FOUR MINUTES TO THE NORTH AND NORTHWEST BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR THE RINGGOLD CHURCH. TORNADO DAMAGE INCLUDED SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS BROKEN OFF...CEDAR TREES UPROOTED...A NINE TOWER IRRIGATION PIVOT SYSTEM TWISTED AND OVERTURNED...A SMALL STORAGE BUILDING DESTROYED...AND DAMAGE TO THE CHURCH BUILDING.

AT HIGHWAY 23 AND HIGHWAY 83 JUNCTION...VISIBILITY AT TIME OF REPORT WAS FREQUENTLY NEAR ZERO. CONDITIONS HAVE EXISTED FOR APPROXIMATELY ONE HOUR WITH NEAR OR ZERO VISIBILITIES AND DANGEROUS TRAVEL CONDITIONS.
INTERSTATE 80 AND HIGHWAY 30 ARE CLOSED WEST OF BIG SPRINGS DUE TO DUST STORM AND CAR PILE UP. NEBRASKA 26 IS ALSO CLOSED FROM LEWELLEN TO OGALLALA DUE TO REDUCED VISIBILITY.
